claim: UCP (Universal Commerce Protocol) is an open standard for agentic commerce, enabling AI agents to discover products, handle carts, and process payments across retailers without custom per-platform integrations.
actionable: If building AI shopping agents, use UCP to access retailer product catalogs and payment flows; reduces per-retailer engineering work.

claim: Product discoverability is a major unsolved problem in agentic commerce; agents currently use brittle web browsing to find products, but UCP enables structured product discovery.
actionable: For agentic product discovery features, prefer UCP integrations over web scraping when retailers support it; scraping is fragile and triggers rate limits.

claim: Retailers adopting UCP gain access to Google AI Mode, Gemini, and other platforms; the value proposition is that one protocol enables sales across multiple AI platforms.
actionable: If you have an e-commerce business, implementing UCP support gives you visibility in multiple AI platforms at once, not just Google.
